narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students just like you. Your story starts strong with that solid 4th decile ranking in Year 1, but it was your 1st decile standing in Year 3 that stood out as the biggest concern—it painted a picture of grades slipping over time, the strongest signal pulling toward the risk of not passing the bar. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 26, which, while showing potential, fell short of the benchmarks that typically light the path to success for others. Your 1st-year decile added to that narrative of early promise not fully sustained, making the model lean even more toward caution. Coming from a lower-middle family income bracket whispered hints of added pressures that have tripped up similar students before. As an Asian female pursuing part-time law school with your undergraduate GPA of 2.3, these details together sketched a profile where the scales tipped toward a 44% chance of bar exam struggles, despite your clear passion marked by targeting law school. Your gender was the one bright thread gently nudging toward passing, like a quiet vote of confidence. It's heartbreaking to see such dedication met with this outcome, but this prediction reflects how these elements combined to signal higher hurdles ahead. You've got resilience in targeting law—perhaps bolstering those later grades or LSAT prep could rewrite this story next time. We're rooting for you to prove the odds wrong. With empathy, the Admissions Review Team.
